title: Discovering Addition with Objects

sentences.text: Look at these two different groups of objects on the screen.
Can you count how many objects are in each group?
Let's combine these two groups to see how many we have together.
One group has three objects and the other has two objects.
Three plus two equals five objects in total.
Now, let's try adding two new groups of objects together.
Group one has four objects, and group two has three objects.
Count with me: four plus three equals how many?
Great job! You learned how to combine groups to find the total.
Keep practicing addition with different groups of objects.

teacherNotes: Encourage students to visualize the addition process with real objects for better understanding.
